description: ModMedic error pattern database — common Paper/Spigot plugin errors and fixes

patterns.name: Null config value
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to read a config path that doesn't exist in config.yml. Config#getString() returns null for missing paths.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add the missing config path to the plugin's config.yml, or use getString("path", "default") to supply a fallback.

patterns.name: Null config integer
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to read a config integer that doesn't exist or is malformed.
patterns.suggested_fix: Verify the config path exists in config.yml and contains a valid integer. Use getInt("path", 0) to supply a default.

patterns.name: Null config list
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to read a config list that doesn't exist.
patterns.suggested_fix: Verify the list path exists in config.yml. Use getList("path", new ArrayList<>()) to supply a default.

patterns.name: Null player reference
patterns.diagnosis: A player object was null when the plugin tried to call getName() on it. This usually means the player went offline or was never properly fetched.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add a null check before using the player: if (player == null) return; or use Bukkit.getPlayerExact(name) instead of Bukkit.getPlayer(name).

patterns.name: Null world at location
patterns.diagnosis: A Location object had a null world when the plugin tried to access a block at that location. Likely from deserializing a location without saving the world name.
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure the Location's world is set. When deserializing, verify the world still exists with Bukkit.getWorld(worldName).

patterns.name: Null item in inventory slot
patterns.diagnosis: The player's main hand item was null. This usually happens when checking an empty hand without a null check.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the item is null before using it: if (item == null || item.getType() == Material.AIR) return;

patterns.name: Non-living entity cast
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to cast an entity to LivingEntity or Player without checking the type first.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add an instanceof check before casting: if (entity instanceof Player) { Player p = (Player) entity; }

patterns.name: Null metadata value
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin called getMetadata() on an entity or world but found no metadata set. getMetadata() returns an empty list, not null, but .get(0) on an empty list throws.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the metadata list is empty before accessing: if (!entity.hasMetadata(key)) return; List<MetadataValue> values = entity.getMetadata(key);

patterns.name: Removed getItemInHand()
patterns.diagnosis: getItemInHand() was removed in Minecraft 1.16+. The plugin was compiled against an older API.
patterns.suggested_fix: Replace getItemInHand() with getInventory().getItemInMainHand() for the main hand, or getInventory().getItemInOffHand() for the off hand.

patterns.name: Removed ItemStack.getName()
patterns.diagnosis: ItemStack.getName() was removed in 1.13+. The plugin was compiled against an older API.
patterns.suggested_fix: Use ItemMeta.getDisplayName() instead: item.getItemMeta().getDisplayName()

patterns.name: Removed ItemStack.setAmount(int)
patterns.diagnosis: ItemStack constructor with amount changed in newer API versions.
patterns.suggested_fix: Use new ItemStack(Material, int) constructor or item.setAmount(int) method separately.

patterns.name: Removed ThrownPotion class
patterns.diagnosis: ThrownPotion class was changed/removed in newer versions. The entity class for thrown potions changed.
patterns.suggested_fix: Use the new class name or spawn a Potion entity instead. Check the Paper API for the current equivalent.

patterns.name: Missing JDBC driver
patterns.diagnosis: The MySQL JDBC driver is not installed on the server. The plugin requires a database driver library.
patterns.suggested_fix: Place the MySQL Connector/J jar in the server's lib/ folder or the plugin's folder. Download from: https://dev.mysql.com/downloads/connector/j/

patterns.name: Missing SQLite driver
patterns.diagnosis: The SQLite JDBC driver is not available. Paper includes it by default, but some older setups may be missing it.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add the SQLite JDBC jar to the classpath, or ensure you're using a modern Paper build (1.16.5+ includes it).

patterns.name: Plugin class not found
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin's main class was not found inside the jar. The plugin.yml points to a class that doesn't exist, or the jar is corrupted.
patterns.suggested_fix: Verify the 'main:' field in plugin.yml matches the fully qualified class name inside the jar. Rebuild and redeploy the plugin.

patterns.name: Missing dependency library
patterns.diagnosis: A required library is missing. The plugin was compiled against a library not present on the server.
patterns.suggested_fix: Install the required library jar in the plugins/ folder or the server's lib/ folder. Check the plugin's documentation for dependencies.

patterns.name: Compiled with wrong Java version
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin was compiled with a newer Java version than the server is running. Java major version mismatch.
patterns.suggested_fix: Recompile the plugin with --release matching the server's Java version (e.g., Java 21 for Paper 1.21). Or update the server to a newer Java version.

patterns.name: Negative item amount
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to create or set an item with a negative or zero amount.
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure item amounts are always between 1 and 64. Add validation before creating items.

patterns.name: Invalid or removed Material
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin used a Material name that was renamed or removed in this Minecraft version (e.g., STATIONARY_WATER → WATER in 1.13).
patterns.suggested_fix: Update the Material name to the current equivalent. Use Material.matchMaterial(name, false) with legacy name support, or use Material.getMaterial() with the modern namespaced key.

patterns.name: Invalid enchantment level
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to add an enchantment with an invalid level (below 1 or above the max).
patterns.suggested_fix: Clamp enchantment levels to the valid range. Check Enchantment.getMaxLevel() and ensure level ≥ 1.

patterns.name: Chunk not loaded
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to access a chunk that isn't loaded. Operations on unloaded chunks throw this error.
patterns.suggested_fix: Call chunk.isLoaded() before accessing it, or use chunk.load() to load it first. For world operations, ensure the world is loaded with Bukkit.getWorld(name) != null.

patterns.name: World not found
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to access a world that doesn't exist or isn't loaded.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the world exists before accessing: if (Bukkit.getWorld(name) == null) return; Verify the world name is correct and the world folder exists.

patterns.name: Invalid tick value
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin passed an invalid tick value to a scheduler or potion effect (e.g., negative duration).
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure tick durations are positive. Add validation before scheduling tasks or creating potion effects.

patterns.name: Already cancelled task
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to cancel a scheduler task that was already cancelled or completed.
patterns.suggested_fix: Track the task ID and only cancel if it's not null and not already cancelled. Check task.isCancelled() before cancelling.

patterns.name: Config save failed
patterns.diagnosis: Failed to save the config file. The file may be locked by another process, or the disk is full.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check file permissions and disk space. Ensure no other process has the config file open. Try saveConfig() again after a short delay.

patterns.name: Invalid world border size
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to set a world border with an invalid size (e.g., negative or too large).
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure the world border size is between 1 and 60000000. Add validation before setting the border.

patterns.name: Concurrent modification
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin modified a collection while iterating over it (e.g., removing entities from a list while looping).
patterns.suggested_fix: Use an Iterator with iterator.remove(), or collect items to remove in a separate list and remove them after the loop.

patterns.name: Database connection timeout
patterns.diagnosis: The database connection timed out. The database server may be down, the connection string may be wrong, or a firewall is blocking the port.
patterns.suggested_fix: Verify the database server is running and reachable. Check the connection string in the plugin config. Ensure the port (default 3306 for MySQL) is open.

patterns.name: Invalid YAML syntax
patterns.diagnosis: The config.yml has invalid YAML syntax (tab instead of spaces, missing colon, etc.). The plugin failed to load.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check the config.yml for YAML errors. Use an online YAML validator. Never use tabs — only spaces. Ensure colons are followed by a space.

patterns.name: Config file missing
patterns.diagnosis: A config file the plugin expects was not found. Some plugins require a config to be created manually.
patterns.suggested_fix: Restart the server to let the plugin generate the default config, or manually create the config.yml in the plugin's folder.

patterns.name: Invalid player name
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin called getOfflinePlayer() with a null or invalid player name.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add a null check for the player name before calling getOfflinePlayer(). Bukkit.getOfflinePlayer() does not throw but may behave unexpectedly with null.

patterns.name: Null world in Location
patterns.diagnosis: A Location object was created without setting its world, or the world was unloaded.
patterns.suggested_fix: Always set the world when creating a Location: new Location(world, x, y, z). When loading from config, verify the world exists with Bukkit.getWorld(name).

patterns.name: Failed entity spawn
patterns.diagnosis: The entity spawn returned null. This can happen if the location is invalid, the chunk isn't loaded, or the entity type doesn't exist.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check that the location's world is loaded, the chunk is loaded, and the entity type is valid for this Minecraft version.

patterns.name: Null scoreboard tag
patterns.diagnosis: The server's scoreboard was null or the plugin tried to add a tag to a non-existent team.
patterns.suggested_fix: Use Bukkit.getScoreboardManager().getMainScoreboard() and check for null. Ensure teams exist before adding players with getTeam(name) != null checks.

patterns.name: Unknown potion effect type
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin used a PotionEffectType name that was renamed or doesn't exist in this version (e.g., CONFUSION → NAUSEA, JUMP → JUMP_BOOST).
patterns.suggested_fix: Update renamed potion effect types: CONFUSION → NAUSEA, JUMP → JUMP_BOOST, SLOW → SLOWNESS, FAST_DIGGING → HASTE.

patterns.name: Remove potion effect type
patterns.diagnosis: A PotionEffectType constant no longer exists or was renamed. Common renames in 1.20.5+ affect many effect types.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check the current Paper API documentation for the correct PotionEffectType name. The constants follow Minecraft registry names.

patterns.name: Wrong entity cast
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to cast an entity to a type it isn't (e.g., casting a Skeleton to a Creeper).
patterns.suggested_fix: Use instanceof checks before casting. Entity types may have been renamed between Minecraft versions (e.g., PIG_ZOMBIE → ZOMBIFIED_PIGLIN).

patterns.name: Removed EntityType
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin used an EntityType that was removed or renamed. Common renames: MUSHROOM_COW → MOOSHROOM, SNOWMAN → SNOW_GOLEM, PIG_ZOMBIE → ZOMBIFIED_PIGLIN.
patterns.suggested_fix: Update to the modern EntityType name. Check Paper API for the current name.

patterns.name: Console-only command used by player
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin assumed a command sender was always a Player. When run from console, the cast fails.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if sender instanceof Player before casting. If not, send a message telling the user this command must be run in-game.

patterns.name: Removed CreatureType
patterns.diagnosis: CreatureType was removed in 1.11+. The plugin needs to use EntityType instead.
patterns.suggested_fix: Replace CreatureType with EntityType. Use world.spawn(location, EntityType.ZOMBIE.getEntityClass()) instead of world.spawnCreature(location, CreatureType.ZOMBIE).

patterns.name: Removed spawnCreature()
patterns.diagnosis: World.spawnCreature() was removed in 1.11+. The plugin needs to use World.spawn() instead.
patterns.suggested_fix: Replace world.spawnCreature(loc, type) with world.spawn(loc, EntityType.ZOMBIE.getEntityClass()).

patterns.name: Cancel null task
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to cancel a task that was never scheduled (task ID was never assigned or BukkitTask is null).
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the task is null before cancelling: if (task != null) task.cancel();

patterns.name: Null FireworkMeta
patterns.diagnosis: The item was not a firework, so getFireworkMeta() returned null.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check that the item's type is Material.FIREWORK_ROCKET before retrieving its FireworkMeta.

patterns.name: Invalid spawn location
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to spawn an entity at an invalid location (e.g., outside the world border or in an unloaded chunk).
patterns.suggested_fix: Validate the location before spawning: check world != null, chunk.isLoaded(), and location is within the world border.

patterns.name: Null command registration
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to register a command executor for a command that doesn't exist in plugin.yml.
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure the command is listed in plugin.yml under the 'commands:' section with the exact same name.

patterns.name: Null player inventory
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to close a null inventory or called closeInventory() on a null player.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the player is online and not null before calling closeInventory().

patterns.name: Null ItemMeta display name
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin called getDisplayName() on null ItemMeta, or the item itself was null.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add null checks: if (item == null || item.getItemMeta() == null) return;

patterns.name: Null open inventory
patterns.diagnosis: The player's open inventory was null. This can happen if the player logged out while an inventory was open.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the player is online before accessing their inventory. Wrap in a try-catch or add null checks.

patterns.name: Null ChatColor formatting
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin passed a null string to ChatColor.translateAlternateColorCodes() or a similar color method.
patterns.suggested_fix: Ensure the string is not null before applying color codes. Add a null check before the translate call.

patterns.name: Invalid Material enum
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to use a Material value that doesn't exist in this Minecraft version. Materials were renamed in 1.13 (The Flattening).
patterns.suggested_fix: Use Material.matchMaterial(name) which returns null for invalid names, or use the namespaced key format (minecraft:stone).

patterns.name: Entity projectile cast error
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to cast a Projectile to Arrow, but it was a different projectile type (trident, snowball, etc.).
patterns.suggested_fix: Use instanceof Arrow check before casting, or check the projectile type with instanceof checks for each type you handle.

patterns.name: plugin.yml load warning
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin's plugin.yml is missing a required field such as 'name', 'version', or 'main'.
patterns.suggested_fix: Verify plugin.yml contains: name, version, main (fully qualified class path), and api-version for modern servers.

patterns.name: Missing api-version in plugin.yml
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin is missing the 'api-version' field in plugin.yml. Paper requires it for plugins targeting 1.13+.
patterns.suggested_fix: Add 'api-version: 1.21' (or your target version) to plugin.yml. This tells Paper which API version the plugin was compiled against.

patterns.name: Null difficulty
patterns.diagnosis: The world's difficulty returned null. This can happen on first world load or when the world data is corrupted.
patterns.suggested_fix: Set the difficulty in server.properties or server.yml, or check for null before using the difficulty value.

patterns.name: Null BlockData
patterns.diagnosis: The block at the location had no BlockData. This can happen if the chunk is unloaded or the location is outside the world.
patterns.suggested_fix: Check if the chunk is loaded and the location is within the world's bounds before calling getBlockData().

patterns.name: Null inventory title
patterns.diagnosis: The plugin tried to get the title of an inventory that was created without one, or the inventory view was closed.
patterns.suggested_fix: Set a title when creating the inventory with Bukkit.createInventory(owner, size, title). Check for null before calling getTitle().
